signals.reasoning: BTC is still stable-to-firm and does not offer a clean enough edge to override the active veto.
ETH remains strong and already moving, which keeps it in watch-only territory rather than a fresh entry.
SOL still reads as a short-covering bounce rather than a clean trend entry in either direction.
ALT remains the best squeeze-long candidate on the sheet because funding is still significantly negative while price is only modestly up, but liquidity quality keeps confidence restrained.
LAYER has strong negative funding too, but liquidity is too poor to trust for a serious fresh position under veto.
GOAT no longer looks like a strong enough crowded fade to deserve fresh risk here.
PUMP has enough liquidity to watch, but the setup is still too middling to override the event brake.
WLD is strong but not especially crowded, so it stays watch-only.
PENGU has upside momentum but no clean rejection or crowding edge, so I would not force it.
VVV remains the best fade on this board because the move is extended and still looks more like short covering than durable trend support.
TON is still a good future peak-rejection watch, but it has not actually cracked yet, so I would keep it watch-only.
